issues.figmaValue: Outline button (white border, transparent/charcoal bg, white text) = btn-secondary
issues.implementationValue: Filled button (white bg, charcoal text) = btn-primary (ThemeHeading assigns index 0 = primary)
issues.difference: Wrong button style: filled vs outline
issues.observation: In the Figma mobile screenshot (node 785:34152), the button 'EXPLORE MORE STUDENT VOICES' is clearly an outline button with a white border and charcoal/transparent background. In the implementation, ThemeHeading.php line 104 assigns variation via $buttonVariations[$i % 3] where index 0 = 'primary'. The btn-primary style in a .dark context renders as a white filled button, which visually contradicts the Figma design.
issues.recommendation: The GalleryCarousel block needs to override the default ThemeHeading button variation cycling. Options: (1) Pass a 'buttonVariation' override prop to ThemeHeading that forces 'secondary' for all buttons, or (2) Change ThemeHeading.php to accept a custom $buttonVariations array, or (3) Store the variation in the button attribute and have ThemeHeading respect it. The simplest approach for this block would be adding a 'buttonVariation' param to ThemeHeading.

files.context: ThemeHeading get_template_part call - needs to pass button variation override
Button variation assignment - needs to support override parameter

issues.verification: Button should appear as outline style (white border, no fill) in the dark card context, matching btn-secondary appearance

issues.figmaValue: Controls (progress bar + nav arrows) positioned BELOW the carousel image in Figma desktop (node 634:40603)
issues.implementationValue: Controls positioned ABOVE the carousel image (between header and carousel)
issues.difference: Controls position differs from Figma desktop but matches spec requirement
issues.observation: The Figma desktop design (node 634:40603) clearly shows the progress bar and navigation arrows below the image. However, implementation spec decision DEC-001 explicitly states: 'Controls go BETWEEN the header and carousel on ALL breakpoints (above the image)' - resolved by spec. The implementation correctly follows the spec. The Figma mobile design (node 785:34152) shows controls above the image, matching the implementation. This is documented as a known spec-vs-Figma deviation.
issues.recommendation: No action needed. The implementation correctly follows the spec override (DEC-001). If the client prefers the Figma desktop layout (controls below image), this would need to be a responsive change: controls above on mobile, below on desktop.

issues.figmaValue: gap: 16px (var(--gap/gap-3, 16px)) between nav arrows at mobile
issues.implementationValue: gap-2 (8px) on mobile, gap-4 (16px) at md+ breakpoint
issues.observation: The Figma mobile design context (node 785:34168) shows gap-[var(--gap/gap-3,16px)] between the navigation arrows. The implementation uses 'gap-2 md:gap-4' which gives 8px on mobile instead of the Figma's 16px. This is visible in the mobile screenshot where the arrow buttons appear closer together than in the Figma mobile design.
issues.recommendation: Change the navigation container gap from 'gap-2 md:gap-4' to 'gap-4' (16px at all breakpoints) to match the Figma design.
issues.verification: Nav arrow buttons should have 16px gap between them at all viewport widths

issues.figmaValue: gap: 32px (var(--gap/gap-5, 32px)) between progress bar and navigation arrows
issues.implementationValue: gap-4 (16px) between progress bar and navigation arrows
issues.observation: The Figma design context for both desktop (node 634:40564) and mobile (node 785:34165) shows the controls row using gap-[var(--gap/gap-5,32px)] between the progress bar and navigation buttons. The implementation uses 'gap-4' (16px) in the controls row container. This 16px difference is clearly visible in the screenshots - the progress bar and arrows are closer together than in Figma.
issues.recommendation: Change the controls row gap from 'gap-4' to 'gap-8' (32px) to match the Figma design.
issues.verification: 32px gap between progress bar and navigation arrow group at all breakpoints

issues.observation: The Figma desktop heading uses 56px Anton font. The theme's text-header-2 token resolves to 48px at base and 60px at sm+ breakpoint (>= 768px). The 4px difference (60px vs 56px) is within semantic tolerance. This was pre-approved in implementation spec decision DEC-004.
issues.recommendation: No action needed. 4px difference is within tolerance and matches existing block patterns (FullWidthMedia, CardsCarousel).

issues.observation: The Figma shows the progress bar track background at 50% white opacity. The implementation uses 20% white opacity, following the existing CardsCarousel convention. This was pre-approved in implementation spec decision DEC-014, which prioritizes consistency with the existing carousel pattern over exact Figma match.
issues.recommendation: No action needed. Decision DEC-014 resolved this by convention - follow existing CardsCarousel pattern for consistency.

passedChecks.details: Section > container > dark card wrapper structure matches Figma. Charcoal background, rounded corners, noise texture overlay, header section, carousel section all present and correctly ordered.
Implementation uses rounded-3xl (24px). Figma shows 25px. 1px difference is within tolerance. Pre-approved in DEC-011.
Implementation uses sm:px-8 sm:py-24 (32px sides, 96px top/bottom). Figma desktop shows px-32px py-96px. Exact match.
Implementation uses px-[18px] py-9 (18px sides, 36px top/bottom). Figma mobile shows px-18px py-36px (py from var(--gap/gap-6, 36px)). Exact match.
Implementation uses sm:py-16 (64px). Figma desktop shows py-64px (var(--margin/margin-5, 64px)). Exact match.
Implementation uses py-6 (24px). Figma mobile shows py-24px (var(--margin/margin-4, 24px)). Exact match.
Implementation uses sm:gap-16 (64px). Figma desktop shows gap-64px (var(--gap/gap-8, 64px)). Exact match.
Implementation uses gap-8 (32px). Figma mobile shows gap-32px (var(--gap/gap-5, 32px)). Exact match.

ThemeHeading columns=2 produces md:grid-cols-2 gap-x-20 layout. Figma shows two columns with eyebrow+heading left, description+button right. Implementation screenshot at 1440px confirms two-column layout is rendering correctly.
ThemeHeading columns=2 stacks to single column below md breakpoint. Mobile screenshot shows stacked layout matching Figma mobile.
Implementation uses sm:aspect-[2.45]. Figma desktop shows 1312x536px = 2.45:1 ratio. Matches.
Implementation uses aspect-[1.08]. Figma mobile shows 318x294px = 1.08:1 ratio. Matches.
Implementation uses rounded-2xl (16px) on image and container. Visible in screenshots.

Implementation uses CSS SVG feTurbulence filter with mix-blend-color-dodge at 8% opacity. Figma shows noise texture at 8% opacity with color-dodge blend mode. The subtle grainy texture is visible in the dark card background of screenshots.
Figma shows Roboto Medium 14px uppercase with line-height 1.1. ThemeHeading eyebrow uses 'uppercase text-sm font-medium leading-[1.1]'. text-sm = 14px. Exact match.

Figma shows Roboto Medium 16px with line-height 1.5. Theme body font is General Sans (not Roboto) but body text uses text-base (16px) with appropriate line-height. Font family difference is a theme-level choice.

Implementation uses carousel-nav-btn which applies w-10 h-10 md:w-12 md:h-12 (40px mobile, 48px desktop). Figma shows size-[40px] for both breakpoints. The mobile matches exactly. Desktop shows 48px vs Figma's 40px - however this follows the shared carousel-nav-btn pattern for consistency.
Implementation uses h-1 (4px). Figma shows h-[4px]. Exact match.

Uses Swiper progressbar type with CSS override in swiper.css. Gradient goes from --gradient-left (neon-green) to --gradient-right (blue), matching Figma's linear-gradient(90deg, rgb(184,255,52) 0%, rgb(71,203,242) 100%).
slidesPerView: 1, spaceBetween: 0, proper module loading (Navigation, Pagination, Autoplay, A11y). Frontend JS reads data attributes correctly. Matches spec.
Nav buttons have proper aria-label and title attributes. Noise texture overlay has aria-hidden='true'. Swiper A11y module loaded.

Controls row only renders when showPagination or showNavigation is true. Individual controls render based on their respective toggle. Verified in screenshots: 'No Controls' variation shows no controls row, 'Pagination Only' shows only progress bar, 'Navigation Only' shows only arrows.
Card has 'dark' class applied which sets white text color. bg-charcoal matches Figma's #1e1e1c (theme charcoal = rgba(31,31,29,1) = #1f1f1d, 1 value difference which is imperceptible).
block.json has 'parent': ['takt/gallery-carousel']. Correctly restricts GalleryCarouselItem to only be used inside GalleryCarousel.

All 9 test variations are visible in the full-page screenshots: Full Content, Minimal, No Controls, Pagination Only, Navigation Only, Long Content, Autoplay/Loop, Single Image, Many Images. Each renders with appropriate content and control visibility.

figma.desktop: Figma node 938:2425 (includes both desktop and mobile frames)

tsxPhpParity.details: GalleryCarousel.tsx and GalleryCarousel.php use identical class names and conditional logic. Child block GalleryCarouselItem.tsx and GalleryCarouselItem.php also match. All cn()/class_name() calls have the same conditional structure.

blockJson.details: Both parent and child block.json files match the implementation spec. Attributes, types, defaults all correct. Child has parent restriction.

frontendJs.details: GalleryCarousel.js correctly reads data attributes, initializes Swiper with matching config (slidesPerView: 1, modules, navigation/pagination selectors). Uses unique selector 'gallery-carousel-swiper'.

swiperCssIntegration.details: Uses shared carousel-nav-btn CSS class and swiper-pagination progressbar gradient from swiper.css. No custom CSS overrides needed.

actionItems.summary: Fix button variation to use btn-secondary (outline) instead of btn-primary (filled) in dark card context
actionItems.notes: Requires either modifying ThemeHeading to support a buttonVariation override param, or finding another way to force secondary variation for this block's buttons.

actionItems.summary: Fix controls row gap from gap-4 (16px) to gap-8 (32px)
actionItems.notes: Simple class change in both PHP and TSX files

actionItems.summary: Fix navigation arrow gap from gap-2 md:gap-4 to gap-4 (16px at all sizes)
actionItems.notes: Simple class change in both PHP and TSX files
